name: 49_Explainable_AI_LIME_Titanic_ML
query: Habari! Ninatafuta kuunda mradi unaoelezea utabiri wa modeli kwa kutumia LIME, hasa na seti ya data ya utabiri wa kuokoka kwa Titanic. Kwanza, pakia seti ya data katika `src/data_loader.py`. Kisha, fundisha Random Forest classifier na uihifadhi chini ya `models/saved_models/`? Hatimaye, tumia LIME kuelezea utabiri wa Random Forest classifier na utekeleze katika `src/visualize.py`. Tengeneza ripoti ikijumuisha maelezo na uihifadhi kama `results/model_explanation.md`. Ripoti inapaswa kujengwa kwa kutumia ama Dash au Bokeh, ikitekelezwa katika `src/report.py`, ili watumiaji waweze kuchunguza jinsi vipengele tofauti vinavyoathiri utabiri wa modeli. Maelezo yanapaswa kuwa wazi na rahisi kueleweka kwa watu wasio wa kiufundi. Zaidi ya hayo, hifadhi mchoro wa umuhimu wa vipengele ulio na lebo nzuri na wa kueleweka katika `results/figures/feature_importance.png`. Asante!

requirements.criteria: Seti ya data ya utabiri wa kuokoka kwa "Titanic" inapakiwa katika `src/data_loader.py`.
"Random Forest classifier" inafundishwa kwa ajili ya utabiri wa kuokoka.
"LIME" inatumika kwa maelezo ya utabiri wa modeli na inatekelezwa katika `src/visualize.py`.
Ripoti ya maelezo ya utabiri wa modeli inatengenezwa na kuhifadhiwa kama `results/model_explanation.md`.
Mchoro wa umuhimu wa vipengele unahifadhiwa kama `results/figures/feature_importance.png`.
Ripoti ya maingiliano inayoonyesha athari za vipengele tofauti kwenye utabiri inatengenezwa kwa kutumia "Dash" au "Bokeh" na inatekelezwa katika `src/report.py`.

preferences.criteria: Ripoti ya maelezo inapaswa kuandikwa kwa mtindo ulio wazi na rahisi, ikifanya iweze kueleweka hata kwa wale wasio na ujuzi wa kina wa kiufundi.
Mchoro wa umuhimu wa vipengele unapaswa kuwa wa kueleweka kwa urahisi, ukiwa na lebo na maelezo wazi.

judge_stats.criteria: Seti ya data ya utabiri wa kuokoka kwa "Titanic" inapakiwa katika `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The `src/data_loader.py` file does not actually load the Titanic dataset. Instead, it contains a placeholder/stub implementation that only returns a dictionary `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` without any real data loading logic. There is no use of pandas, CSV reading, or any actual Titanic dataset (e.g., `titanic.csv`) being loaded. The `load_dataset()` function is explicitly described as a "Minimal dataset loader placeholder" and does not perform any genuine data loading operations.

judge_stats.criteria: "Random Forest classifier" inafundishwa kwa ajili ya utabiri wa kuokoka.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The `data_loader.py` file contains only a placeholder implementation with no actual Random Forest classifier training. The file merely defines a `load_dataset()` function that returns a dummy dictionary `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` and a `build_transforms()` function with placeholder data. There is no evidence of a Random Forest model being trained anywhere in the project — no training script, no model fitting code, and the `models/saved_models` directory appears empty (no saved model files are listed). The workspace structure shows no file (e.g., `train.py` or similar) that would implement Random Forest training for Titanic survival prediction.

judge_stats.criteria: "LIME" inatumika kwa maelezo ya utabiri wa modeli na inatekelezwa katika `src/visualize.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The content of `src/visualize.py` does not implement LIME at all. Instead of actual LIME-based model explanation code, the file contains only a minimal stub/placeholder that defines metadata strings about the requirement (e.g., `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA = ['"LIME" is used for model prediction explanation...']`) and a `main()` function that simply returns a status dictionary. There is no import of the `lime` library, no LIME explainer instantiation, no explanation generation, and no visualization of LIME results. The file is essentially a fake implementation that describes the requirement rather than fulfilling it.

judge_stats.criteria: Ripoti ya maelezo ya utabiri wa modeli inatengenezwa na kuhifadhiwa kama `results/model_explanation.md`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The file `results/model_explanation.md` exists in the workspace, but its content is merely `('placeholder', None)` — which is not a valid model explanation report. A proper report should contain meaningful explanations of model predictions (e.g., LIME explanations, feature importance analysis, interpretation of results). The placeholder content indicates the file was never properly generated or populated with actual model explanation data.

judge_stats.criteria: Mchoro wa umuhimu wa vipengele unahifadhiwa kama `results/figures/feature_importance.png`.

reason: <SATISFIED>: The file `results/figures/feature_importance.png` exists in the project structure as shown in the workspace tree (`results\figures\feature_importance.png`). Although the image content could not be read due to a technical error in the evaluation system (image format mismatch in the API call), the file itself is present at the correct path, satisfying the requirement that the feature importance plot be saved as `results/figures/feature_importance.png`.

judge_stats.criteria: Ripoti ya maingiliano inayoonyesha athari za vipengele tofauti kwenye utabiri inatengenezwa kwa kutumia "Dash" au "Bokeh" na inatekelezwa katika `src/report.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The file `src/report.py` does not implement an interactive report using Dash or Bokeh. Instead, it contains only a minimal stub/placeholder implementation that simply defines some metadata strings (`REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, `KEYWORDS`) and a `main()` function that returns a dictionary with status "ok" — there is no actual Dash or Bokeh code, no interactive visualization, and no feature impact analysis. The trajectory evidence also confirms that no implementation of an interactive report using Dash or Bokeh was ever executed or recorded.

<UNSATISFIED>: The project structure shows that the `models/saved_models/` directory exists but contains no files. No trained model has been saved there. The `data_loader.py` file is merely a placeholder with no actual model training or saving logic, and there is no evidence of any model file (e.g., `.pkl`, `.joblib`, `.h5`, etc.) being stored under `models/saved_models/`.
